question_en: For an array sorted in increasing order and supporting random access, what is the key idea for finding the i-th largest element?

choices.A: It requires scanning the entire array.
choices.B: It requires sorting the array again.
choices.C: It can be obtained by direct indexing from the end of the array.

correct_answer_en: It can be obtained by direct indexing from the end of the array.
explanation_en: In a sorted array, the i-th largest element is at a known position relative to the end, so random access makes the selection constant time.
